description: Public HOMESERVER appliance profile. Harmonia keeps this profile current by running ordered modules and writing receipts.
scaffold_status: public product profile; executable Rust toolchain, Coronatio, Caduceus, git-host group, Jellyfin, and Matrix/Element service modules; remaining homeserver ghosts stay README-only for later slices
public_source_boundary: public reusable product module concerns and non-secret configuration only
runtime_values: credentials, keys, tokens, passwords, and site-specific values are supplied outside public source

toolchain_contract.proof: root PATH resolves /usr/local/bin wrappers and target-native cargo builds pass before binary promotion

managed_file_update_contract.meaning: Managed-file modules own desired files directly in their module directory; Harmonia renders, compares desired bytes to installed bytes, writes only on drift, performs the domain reconcile step, and records receipts.

update_steps: compare desired bytes to installed bytes
write temporary target only on difference
reload UDEV rules when any rule changed
compare desired bytes to installed bytes
write temporary target only on difference
run systemctl daemon-reload when any unit changed
reconcile declared unit enablement or activity

module_currentness_contract.meaning: A module is a self-contained intent that must remain updated.
update_skeleton: prove currentness or first blocker in receipts
module_currentness_contract.tool_boundary: shared tools provide primitives; modules compose them into the update law for one appliance concern

matrix_contract.secret_values: generated once on the appliance when absent; repo owns only paths/modes/presence/validity and never overwrites filled values
